subject: Fix doc build when git submodule is broken
commit_message:
Fix doc build when git submodule is broken

Culprit: ccsdk/cds git submodule

Removed problematic "--depth 1" from git submodule update
in tox.ini due to which the build kept failing.

The build is now potentially slower then with this option,
but it runs successfully and reliably.

Change-Id: I164e45d4958e729fa5ef9eed02986df2b8058226
Issue-ID: DOC-408
Signed-off-by: Petr Ospalý <p.ospaly@partner.samsung.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

Why it needs to pass if git submodule is broken? Should we fix the submodule?

Patch Set 1:

It is broken because of git metadata - the exact reason why in the doc page is said: Wait for the above change to be merged before any merge to the project repository that you have just added as a submodule. If the project repository added as submodule changes before the doc project merge, git may not automatically update the submodule reference on changes and/or the verify job will fail in the step below.
https://docs.onap.org/en/casablanca/guides/onap-developer/how-to-use-docs/include-documentation.html#setup-project-repositories-s

And the reason why we did the freeze - this can be fixed I guess only by removing the submodule and let them do it again correctly

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/doc-master-verify-rtd/4009/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/doc-master-verify-rtd/4009

Patch Set 1:

This is the error I get when I try to build doc/master locally:
warning: redirecting to https://gerrit.onap.org/r/ccsdk/cds/
remote: Total 0 (delta 0), reused 0 (delta 0)        
warning: redirecting to https://gerrit.onap.org/r/ccsdk/cds/
error: Server does not allow request for unadvertised object 933e47233a168840a64e3ac97711a35d0e5731fb
Fetched in submodule path 'docs/submodules/ccsdk/cds.git', but it did not contain 933e47233a168840a64e3ac97711a35d0e5731fb. Direct fetching of that commit failed.
ERROR: InvocationError for command '/usr/bin/git submodule update --depth 1 --init' (exited with code 1)

My fix solves this, or is there another way how you build the doc?

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

The git submodule information obtained from a clone of the doc project should always be valid or you want know that it fails.   In most cases, tox -elocal is testing one or more rst file changes in a contributing repository against the rest of the contributing repository rst files/references.  During the initial set-up of a contributing project as a submodule you could create submodule references that are invalid.  Would some improvements in the instructions for initial setup, including to use tox -edocs instead of -elocal to test initial rst file integration meet your needs?

Patch Set 1:

Hi @Petr Ospaly, Follow below commands. 

git clone https://gerrit.onap.org/r/doc

cd doc

git submodule init

git submodule update     

tox -e local

it works for me.

Patch Set 2:

Hi @Manish Kumar, thanks for that - it directed me to the problematic argument "--depth" - which did some shallow update and it break with that ccsdk/cds submodule. I made a new patchset to deal with this better than in the previous version.

Unfortunately if someone wants to build the doc it must be patient to download very slowly (at least here in Europe) 2,4G of repo data (crazy).

Patch Set 2: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/doc-master-verify-rtd/4032/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/doc-master-verify-rtd/4032

Patch Set 2:

With --depth 1 it is much more faster process and does not spend so much time and space with downloading 2,4+GB of nonsense (vs 700MB with --depth 1). But to make it work I have to remove that broken submodule ccsdk/cds - so that probably would be better change then these two my patchsets so far.

I am puzzled that no one cares - master is broken, the working instructions of Manish Kumar are working in the same way as my second patchset - but it so slow and such a waste of storage.

How does the community build the docs? The official procedure is broken, that is why I have these patchsets here, but original tox.ini seems to be fine - you just must not merge something what breaks master and if you do, fix it right away by reverting it.

I would like @RichBennett to tell me what he thinks.

Thanks

Patch Set 2:

@Petr Ospaly - 

I found the issue causing the tox -elocal to fail.  The doc project references to ccsdk/apps.git, ccsdk/cds.git, oom/offline-installer.git as submodules are not the HEAD of master branch.  Without a --depth 1 option the git submodule update succeeds and with a --depth 1 it fails.   When it succeeds you get older versions of those three submodules published HTML.

I have not had time to confirm the exact cause, but the order of the initial commits on doc repo vs submodule repos could cause this.   There was also a gerrit upgrade that impacted the tracking of submodules for a few weeks of February https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/78448/

I agree shallow clones are more efficient, especially on the ~2 ONAP repos that are large.
I wanted to use depth 1 in the verify job, but Jenkins SCM and/or the JJB scripts do not support the submodule update depth 1.   

I think we want to leave the tox -elocal logic as is detecting a failure on a local test.   

You should continue to use depth 1 for local tests.  

Perhaps we should add a daily documentation test that performs more extensive tests that are expensive or can't be done in a verify job (check HEAD of branch references, validity of URLs, summarizes RST warnings by project, etc).

I will submit a patch to advance the 3 submodule references and add you as reviewer.

Patch Set 2:

@RichBennett ok, great, so, I will abandon this change, thanks!

Abandoned

Will be fixed by Rich Bennett
